subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Trench Foot and Immersion Injuries
description: A micro-lesson that makes Trench Foot and Immersion Injuries usable.

hook.text: WWI soldiers stood in wet trenches for days. Their feet turned black and fell apart. Trench foot.

buildup.text: Trench foot occurs when feet are cold and wet for extended periods—even above freezing.

discovery.text: Prolonged cold moisture damages blood vessels and nerves without forming ice crystals.

twist.text: Trench foot isn't just historical—it still affects homeless people and outdoor workers today.

climax.text: Prevention is simple: dry feet, change socks, elevate periodically. Treatment is much harder.

punchline.text: No ice needed. Just cold, wet, and time. Dry socks prevent a 100-year-old enemy.

quiz.question: What causes trench foot?
options: Prolonged exposure to cold, wet conditions—even above freezing
Walking too much in combat boots
